description: Foundation milestone: Docker Compose orchestration, all service scaffolds (6 services), database schema with all 22 tables, gRPC contracts, message broker setup, production authentication (Azure AD + MSAL), auth bypass for development, frontend scaffold (Vite + React + providers), and complete test infrastructure with CI/CD pipeline.

userStories.title: Docker Compose Development Environment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete Docker Compose setup so that I can run all services locally with hot reload and consistent configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: `docker compose up` starts all services without errors
PostgreSQL container uses pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image with pgvector extension enabled
Redis container running for Django Channels layer
RabbitMQ container with management UI accessible
Nginx reverse proxy routes /api, /ws, and / correctly
All service containers have health checks
Hot reload works for frontend (Vite HMR) and backend (Django autoreload)
Environment variables loaded from .env file
Volume mounts configured for persistent data (DB, PDF storage)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Container Topology — Docker Compose setup with PostgreSQL (pgvector), Redis, RabbitMQ, Nginx. Dev profile with hot reload. docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Monorepo Strategy — all services in single repo.
Testing: No test matrix entries for infrastructure setup itself — this is validated by subsequent stories depending on services running. Runtime safety: no specific IDs, but proper health checks prevent cascading failures.
Files: docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.override.yml (dev), .env.example, nginx/nginx.conf, services/*/Dockerfile
Gotchas: pgvector extension must be available in PostgreSQL image (use pgvector/pgvector:pg16). Nginx routing must handle both HTTP (/api) and WebSocket (/ws) upgrade headers. Volume permissions for PDF storage.

userStories.title: Gateway Service Scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the API Gateway service scaffolded so that it can serve REST endpoints, WebSocket connections, and route to internal services via gRPC.
acceptanceCriteria: Django 5 project created in services/gateway/
Django REST Framework installed and configured
Django Channels installed with ASGI config
Settings split: settings/base.py, settings/dev.py, settings/test.py, settings/prod.py
CORS disabled (reverse proxy handles same-origin)
Health endpoint GET /api/health returns 200 with service status
WebSocket endpoint /ws/ accepts connections (placeholder consumer)
gRPC client stubs directory created (populated in US-006)
Service starts without errors in Docker container
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Microservice Decomposition — Gateway service with Django 5 + DRF + Channels, owns users, notifications tables. docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Backend Stack Details — Django 5, DRF, Channels for WebSocket.
Testing: No test matrix entries yet — feature tests come in later milestones. This story establishes the scaffold.
Files: services/gateway/manage.py, services/gateway/config/settings/, services/gateway/config/asgi.py, services/gateway/config/urls.py, services/gateway/apps/health/views.py, services/gateway/Dockerfile, services/gateway/requirements.txt
Gotchas: ASGI application must support both HTTP and WebSocket protocols. Settings must load environment variables for DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, SECRET_KEY. Health endpoint should check DB connection but not fail startup if DB not ready yet (for container orchestration).

userStories.title: Core Service Scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Core service scaffolded so that it can serve gRPC requests, run Celery workers for background tasks, and own the core domain logic tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Django 5 project created in services/core/
gRPC server module with health check servicer
Celery worker config with Redis broker
Celery beat config for periodic tasks
Settings split: base, dev, test, prod
Django apps created: ideas, chat, board, brd, review, collaboration, similarity, admin_config
gRPC server starts on port 50051 (configurable)
Celery worker starts without errors
Celery beat starts without errors
Health check gRPC call succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Microservice Decomposition — Core service with Django 5, gRPC server, Celery worker + beat. Owns ideas, chat, board, BRD, review, collaboration, merge, keywords tables. docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Task Scheduling — Celery for periodic jobs.
Testing: No test matrix entries for scaffold itself. Runtime safety: LOOP-005 (background keyword matching job), LOOP-008 (soft delete cleanup job) — these jobs implemented in later milestones, but Celery beat setup happens here.
Files: services/core/manage.py, services/core/config/settings/, services/core/grpc_server/__init__.py, services/core/grpc_server/server.py, services/core/celery_app.py, services/core/apps/{ideas,chat,board,brd,review,collaboration,similarity,admin_config}/__init__.py, services/core/Dockerfile, services/core/requirements.txt
Gotchas: gRPC server runs separately from Django (different process). Celery worker needs access to Django ORM. Settings must configure both gRPC port and Celery broker URL. Health check servicer is a placeholder — full servicers in US-006.

userStories.title: AI, Notification, and PDF Service Scaffolds
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the AI, Notification, and PDF services scaffolded so that the system has all 6 services ready for implementation in later milestones.
acceptanceCriteria: AI service: lightweight Django project in services/ai/ with Semantic Kernel installed
AI service: gRPC server with health check servicer
AI service: owns chat_context_summaries, facilitator_context_bucket, context_agent_bucket, context_chunks, idea_embeddings tables (models created in US-005)
Notification service: Python event consumer in services/notification/
Notification service: RabbitMQ consumer setup with event routing
PDF service: Python gRPC server in services/pdf/ with WeasyPrint installed
All three services have health endpoints/checks
All three services start without errors in Docker
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Microservice Decomposition — AI (Django + Semantic Kernel, gRPC), Notification (Python consumer, stateless), PDF (Python + WeasyPrint, gRPC). docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § Orchestration Framework — Semantic Kernel (Python SDK). docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § PDF Generation — WeasyPrint.
Testing: No test matrix entries for scaffolds. Runtime safety: AI service will handle TIMEOUT-001 (AI processing timeout), but implementation is in later milestones.
Files: services/ai/{manage.py,config/settings/,grpc_server/,requirements.txt,Dockerfile}, services/notification/{main.py,consumer.py,requirements.txt,Dockerfile}, services/pdf/{main.py,server.py,requirements.txt,Dockerfile}
Gotchas: AI service needs Semantic Kernel and Azure OpenAI SDK. Notification service runs as standalone event consumer (not Django). PDF service needs WeasyPrint dependencies in Dockerfile. All services need health checks for Docker Compose depends_on conditions.

userStories.title: Database Schema Migrations and Seed Data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all 22 database tables created with correct schemas, indexes, and seed data so that the application has a stable schema from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: All 22 tables created via Django migrations: users, ideas, idea_collaborators, chat_messages, ai_reactions, user_reactions, board_nodes, board_connections, brd_drafts, brd_versions, review_assignments, review_timeline_entries, collaboration_invitations, notifications, chat_context_summaries, idea_keywords, merge_requests, facilitator_context_bucket, context_agent_bucket, admin_parameters, monitoring_alert_configs, context_chunks, idea_embeddings
pgvector extension created in initial migration (CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector)
All indexes from data-model.md created
All CHECK constraints on varchar columns enforced
All UNIQUE constraints created (including partial unique indexes)
All foreign keys with correct ON DELETE behavior (CASCADE where specified, SET NULL where specified)
Seed data migration: 23 admin parameters (application + infrastructure + AI-specific) with defaults
Seed data migration: facilitator_context_bucket singleton row (empty content)
Seed data migration: context_agent_bucket singleton row (empty sections, empty free_text)
Conditional seed data migration: 4 dev users (only when DEBUG=True) with ids, emails, roles
Migration runs successfully with `python manage.py migrate`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § all table definitions, indexes, seed data. docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Database Strategy — shared PostgreSQL, tables owned by services. docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § pgvector extension.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § data integrity tests will validate schema correctness in later milestones. Runtime safety: INTEGRITY-002 (chat message immutability), INTEGRITY-003 (BRD version immutability) — enforced by application layer, but schema designed for append-only.
Files: services/core/apps/{ideas,chat,board,brd,review,collaboration,similarity,admin_config}/migrations/0001_initial.py, services/gateway/apps/authentication/migrations/0001_initial.py (users table), services/gateway/apps/notifications/migrations/0001_initial.py (notifications table), services/ai/apps/context/migrations/0001_initial.py (AI-owned tables), data migration files for seed data
Gotchas: pgvector extension must be created before any table uses vector() type. Partial unique indexes (WHERE clauses) require raw SQL in migrations. context_chunks and idea_embeddings use vector(1536) — dimension from text-embedding-3-small. Dev user seed must check DEBUG=True. Admin parameters include AI-specific params from docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md.

userStories.title: gRPC Protobuf Definitions and Message Broker Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all gRPC contracts defined in .proto files, stubs generated, and message broker exchanges configured so that services can communicate.
acceptanceCriteria: All .proto files from api-design.md created in proto/ directory
gRPC Python stubs generated for all services
Core service implements placeholder gRPC servicers (return empty/default responses)
AI service implements placeholder gRPC servicers
PDF service implements placeholder gRPC servicers
Gateway can call core/AI/PDF servicers and receive responses
RabbitMQ exchanges declared: idea.events, ai.events, notification.events, similarity.events
RabbitMQ queues declared for each consumer with bindings
Dead-letter exchange (DLX) configured for every queue
Notification service connects to RabbitMQ and logs received events (placeholder processing)
Idempotent event consumer base class created with event ID tracking and duplicate detection (NFR-R2)
Health check gRPC calls succeed from gateway to all internal services
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § gRPC service definitions (Gateway→Core, Gateway→AI, Gateway→PDF). docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § gRPC (grpcio + protobuf), Message Broker (RabbitMQ dev, Azure Service Bus prod), Error Handling Strategy — DLQ on all queues. docs/01-requirements/nonfunctional.md § NFR-R1 (failed messages captured), NFR-R2 (idempotent consumers).
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § gRPC contract tests in later milestones (pattern: grpcio-testing). Runtime safety: LOOP-003 (gRPC retry loop) — retry logic added in gateway client wrapper here. INTEGRITY-007 (rate limit counter) — not yet implemented, but event structure defined.
Files: proto/{core,ai,pdf}.proto, services/gateway/grpc_clients/{core,ai,pdf}_client.py, services/core/grpc_server/servicers/, services/ai/grpc_server/servicers/, services/pdf/grpc_server/servicers/, services/notification/consumer.py (idempotent base), scripts/generate_proto_stubs.sh
Gotchas: Proto files must define all message types even if not used in M1 (schema stability). Placeholder servicers should return valid but empty data structures (not errors). DLX configuration requires x-dead-letter-exchange argument on queue declaration. Idempotent consumer base tracks processed event IDs in Redis (keyed by idea_id + event_id) with expiry (24 hours).

userStories.title: Production Authentication (Azure AD + MSAL)
userStories.description: As a user, I want secure authentication via Azure AD so that only Commerz Real employees can access the application in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend: @azure/msal-react installed and configured
Frontend: MSAL provider wraps app with Azure AD tenant config (AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID, AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID from env)
Frontend: Login page at /login with Azure AD redirect
Frontend: Silent token refresh before expiry implemented
Frontend: Expired token redirects to /login
Frontend: All routes protected — unauthenticated users redirected to /login
Backend: Azure AD token validation middleware for REST endpoints (Bearer token from Authorization header)
Backend: Azure AD token validation middleware for WebSocket (token from query param /ws/?token=<jwt>)
Backend: JWKS key caching for token validation
Backend: User claims extraction (object ID, roles from AD group membership)
Backend: User sync to shadow users table (upsert on first validated request per session)
POST /api/auth/validate endpoint validates token and returns user object
Authenticated request to protected endpoint succeeds (200)
Unauthenticated request to protected endpoint returns 401
WebSocket connection with valid token succeeds
WebSocket connection without token or invalid token rejected
User with 'admin' group in AD has 'admin' role in users.roles array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Authentication — MSAL.js (frontend), Azure AD token validation (backend). docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Authentication endpoints (POST /api/auth/validate). docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § users table — shadow table synced from Azure AD. docs/01-requirements/nonfunctional.md § NFR-S1 through NFR-S3, NFR-S6, NFR-S8.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § F-7.2 (production auth) — no specific test IDs in M1, but integration tests for auth middleware must verify token validation, user sync, role assignment. docs/04-test-architecture/testing-strategy.md § Pattern 1: Testing Authenticated Endpoints.
Files: frontend/src/auth/msal-config.ts, frontend/src/auth/MsalProvider.tsx, frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx, services/gateway/apps/authentication/middleware.py (REST), services/gateway/apps/websocket/middleware.py (WebSocket), services/gateway/apps/authentication/views.py (validate endpoint), services/gateway/apps/authentication/utils.py (JWKS caching, claims extraction)
Gotchas: Azure AD app registration must be pre-configured with correct redirect URIs and API permissions. Roles derived from AD group membership (map group object IDs to role strings). Silent token refresh happens in frontend via MSAL (backend just validates). WebSocket token passed as query param (not header) due to browser WebSocket API limitations. User sync must be upsert (UPDATE if exists, INSERT if new) based on id (Azure AD object ID).

userStories.title: Development Auth Bypass and Dev User Switcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an auth bypass mode with 4 preconfigured dev users so that I can test all user roles locally without Azure AD dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth bypass double-gated: requires AUTH_BYPASS=True AND DEBUG=True in settings
Auth bypass cannot activate in production (settings validation blocks it)
4 dev users seeded in database (from US-005): Dev User 1 (user role), Dev User 2 (user role), Dev User 3 (user+reviewer roles), Dev User 4 (user+admin roles)
GET /api/auth/dev-users endpoint returns list of dev users (only in bypass mode, otherwise 404)
POST /api/auth/dev-login endpoint accepts {user_id} and sets session (only in bypass mode, otherwise 404)
POST /api/auth/validate endpoint works in bypass mode (reads from session instead of validating Azure token)
Frontend: DevAuthProvider component wraps app when AUTH_BYPASS detected
Frontend: User switcher in navbar (only visible in bypass mode) allows switching between 4 dev users
Frontend: No /login page shown in bypass mode (users select from navbar switcher)
All permission checks work identically to production (bypass only skips Azure AD token validation)
Dev User 3 can access /reviews page (reviewer role)
Dev User 4 can access /admin page (admin role)
Dev User 1 and 2 cannot access /reviews or /admin (no roles)
Attempting to activate bypass with AUTH_BYPASS=True but DEBUG=False raises error on startup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Authentication (Backend) — auth bypass for local dev. docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Authentication endpoints (dev-login, dev-users). docs/01-requirements/features.md § F-7.1 (dev auth bypass). docs/01-requirements/nonfunctional.md § NFR-S4 (double-gate enforcement).
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § F-7.1 test IDs not explicitly listed for M1, but E2E test infrastructure (US-010) will use bypass mode. docs/04-test-architecture/testing-strategy.md § Test Environment Configuration — AUTH_BYPASS=true for E2E tests.
Files: services/gateway/apps/authentication/views.py (dev-users, dev-login), services/gateway/apps/authentication/middleware.py (bypass mode detection), frontend/src/auth/DevAuthProvider.tsx, frontend/src/components/DevUserSwitcher.tsx, services/gateway/config/settings/base.py (bypass validation)
Gotchas: Double-gate must be enforced in settings startup (raise ImproperlyConfigured if AUTH_BYPASS without DEBUG). Session-based auth in bypass mode (not token-based). Dev users are real rows in users table (seeded in US-005) with hardcoded UUIDs and roles. All middleware permission checks must work identically — bypass is transparent to application logic. Frontend detects bypass mode via absence of AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID env var or presence of AUTH_BYPASS flag.

userStories.title: Frontend React Scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend scaffolded with Vite, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Redux Toolkit, TanStack Query, and all route definitions so that UI implementation can begin in later milestones.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite 7 project created in frontend/ with TypeScript 5
React 19 installed
Tailwind CSS 4 configured (CSS-native config, no tailwind.config.ts)
shadcn/ui initialized with theme system (light/dark mode CSS variables)
Redux Toolkit store created with empty slices (board, websocket, ui)
Redux DevTools enabled in development
TanStack Query client configured with default options
React Router configured with routes: /, /idea/:uuid, /reviews, /admin, /login
Each route renders a placeholder page component
MSAL provider wraps app (from US-007)
DevAuthProvider wraps app conditionally (from US-008)
TanStack Query provider wraps app
Redux provider wraps app
Gotham font files self-hosted in public/fonts/ with @font-face declarations
Lucide React icons installed
react-i18next installed with en.json and de.json (empty placeholder files)
Theme switcher component in navbar (toggles dark class on html)
Language switcher component in navbar (changes i18next language)
Nginx routes / to Vite dev server, /api to gateway, /ws to gateway WebSocket
Frontend loads at http://localhost without errors
Navigating to /idea/test-uuid renders IdeaWorkspace placeholder
Typecheck passes with tsc --noEmit
ESLint passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Frontend Stack Details — React 19, Vite 7, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Redux Toolkit, TanStack Query, MSAL. docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Frontend structure. docs/03-design/design-system.md § Brand & Typography — Gotham font. docs/01-requirements/pages.md § Route Overview.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § no M1 tests for UI (feature tests in later milestones). docs/04-test-architecture/testing-strategy.md § Vitest for frontend unit tests (configured in US-010).
Files: frontend/{package.json,vite.config.ts,tsconfig.json,index.html,src/main.tsx,src/App.tsx,src/store/index.ts,src/routes/index.tsx,src/pages/{LandingPage,IdeaWorkspace,ReviewPage,AdminPanel,LoginPage}.tsx,src/auth/{MsalProvider,DevAuthProvider}.tsx,src/components/{ThemeSwitcher,LanguageSwitcher}.tsx,public/fonts/gotham/,tailwind.css,src/i18n/locales/{en,de}.json}
Gotchas: Tailwind CSS 4 uses CSS-native config (no JS config file). Gotham font requires commercial license (assume files provided). shadcn/ui theme variables in globals.css (:root and .dark). Redux store is minimal — slices populated in later milestones. Route components are placeholders (div with page name). MSAL provider requires env vars (VITE_AZURE_AD_TENANT_ID, VITE_AZURE_AD_CLIENT_ID). Nginx config must handle WebSocket upgrade headers for /ws.

userStories.title: Test Infrastructure and CI/CD Pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want complete test infrastructure (factories, helpers, fixtures) and a CI/CD pipeline so that all code is tested before merging.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend: factory-boy factories for all 18 entities (users, ideas, chat_messages, board_nodes, etc.) with sensible defaults
Backend: MockGrpcClient fixture for testing gateway without running core/AI/PDF services
Backend: MockBrokerPublisher fixture for testing event publishing
Backend: AuthenticatedAPIClient fixture for REST endpoint tests
Backend: pytest.ini configured with Django settings module, coverage options
Backend: pytest-django installed and configured
Frontend: renderWithProviders helper (wraps component with Redux, TanStack Query, Router, Auth providers)
Frontend: createTestStore helper for Redux tests
Frontend: mockAuthUser helper for auth state
Frontend: All fixture builders for UI state (ideas, chat messages, board nodes, etc.)
Frontend: Vitest configured (vite.config.ts test section)
Frontend: React Testing Library installed
E2E: docker-compose.test.yml with test database and AI mock mode
E2E: Playwright installed and configured
E2E: scripts/seed-e2e.py creates baseline test data (4 dev users, 3 ideas in various states)
E2E: AI service AI_MOCK_MODE=True returns fixture responses
CI: GitHub Actions workflow (or equivalent) with 4 stages: (1) Lint + typecheck, (2) Unit tests per service, (3) E2E tests, (4) Coverage reports
CI: Stage 1 runs ESLint, tsc, Ruff, mypy in parallel
CI: Stage 2 runs pytest (backend) and Vitest (frontend) in parallel
CI: Stage 3 runs Playwright E2E tests sequentially after docker-compose.test.yml starts
CI: Coverage reports uploaded and PR comments generated
CI: All stages must pass to merge (gates enforced)
Run `pytest services/gateway/` succeeds (even with no tests yet — infrastructure validated)
Run `npm test` in frontend/ succeeds (even with no tests yet)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/testing-strategy.md § entire document — factory pattern, test organization, CI pipeline stages. docs/04-test-architecture/test-plan.md § test infrastructure requirements. docs/04-test-architecture/test-fixtures.md § fixture design.
Testing: This story IS the test infrastructure. Runtime safety: Tests for runtime safety specs (docs/04-test-architecture/runtime-safety.md) will be written in later milestones using this infrastructure.
Files: services/gateway/tests/factories.py, services/core/tests/factories.py, services/gateway/tests/conftest.py (fixtures), services/core/tests/conftest.py, services/gateway/pytest.ini, services/core/pytest.ini, frontend/src/test-utils/{render,store,auth}.tsx, frontend/src/test-utils/fixtures.ts, frontend/vitest.config.ts, e2e/playwright.config.ts, docker-compose.test.yml, scripts/seed-e2e.py, .github/workflows/ci.yml (or equivalent CI config)
Gotchas: factory-boy factories must handle FK relationships (SubFactory, RelatedFactory). MockGrpcClient must stub all servicer methods used in tests. docker-compose.test.yml overrides AI service env (AI_MOCK_MODE=True) and uses separate test DB. Playwright config must wait for services to be healthy before running tests (wait-on script). Coverage thresholds from testing-strategy.md: backend services 80%, frontend hooks 70%. E2E seed script must be idempotent (can run multiple times). AI mock mode returns fixture JSON files from services/ai/fixtures/.
